blocks.text: NOTE : Buy the absolute freshest corn you can find, and use it the day you bring it home.

SERVES 6 6 ears corn, husks and silks removed 6 cups homemade or low-sodium canned chicken stock 1 bay leaf 1 teaspoon fennel seeds 1 teaspoon coriander seeds 1 teaspoon whole black peppercorns 4 ounces salt pork or slab bacon, cut into 1/2-inch cubes (optional) 3 tablespoons unsalted butter 1 medium onion, finely diced (about 1 cup) 2 medium cloves garlic, minced or grated on a Microplane (about 2 teaspoons) 1 to 2 russet (baking) potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch dice (about 11/2 cups) Kosher salt 2 cups whole milk or half-and-half Freshly ground black pepper Sugar (if necessary) 3 scallions, finely sliced

canonical_text:
THE BEST CORN CHOWDER
NOTE : Buy the absolute freshest corn you can find, and use it the day you bring it home.
SERVES 6 6 ears corn, husks and silks removed 6 cups homemade or low-sodium canned chicken stock 1 bay leaf 1 teaspoon fennel seeds 1 teaspoon coriander seeds 1 teaspoon whole black peppercorns 4 ounces salt pork or slab bacon, cut into 1/2-inch cubes (optional) 3 tablespoons unsalted butter 1 medium onion, finely diced (about 1 cup) 2 medium cloves garlic, minced or grated on a Microplane (about 2 teaspoons) 1 to 2 russet (baking) potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch dice (about 11/2 cups) Kosher salt 2 cups whole milk or half-and-half Freshly ground black pepper Sugar (if necessary) 3 scallions, finely sliced
